panel.strength: The plan correctly recognizes that the intended-use positioning (autonomous diagnosis vs. screening-triage with mandatory over-read) is unresolved and materially changes the evidentiary bar, and defers claims language to FDA engagement.

critiques.issue: The primary intended-use claim remains inferred and the autonomous-vs-triage question is unresolved; the entire evidentiary burden and comparator cannot be locked until this is fixed.
critiques.evidence_basis: The plan itself flags the claim as 'inferred — team to confirm' in the Intended Clinical Claim and Field 7; no clearance or claim language is anchored in retrieved evidence.

critiques.issue: No AI DR-grader device precedent or applicable product code/pathway is established; the plan references a De Novo autonomous DR-AI pathway that was NOT retrieved and must not be relied upon as anchored.
critiques.evidence_basis: Retrieved openFDA ophthalmic records are physical cameras/lenses only (Hruby lens HJI reg 886.1395; Canon CF-60UV K901839; Yannuzzi K864304); no software-classifier precedent appears, and the plan concedes the De Novo pathway 'must be independently verified.'

critiques.issue: The retrieval explicitly did not query non-US regulators or all FDA endpoints, so the predicate/pathway landscape is a floor, not complete; the plan should state this limits any equivalence argument.
critiques.evidence_basis: Coverage & Retrieval Gaps note absence of EMA/MHRA/PMDA and that 'Absence of a record above is NOT evidence of absence.'

panel.strength: The plan appropriately targets bounding the lower confidence limit of referable-DR sensitivity at a pre-specified floor and insists sizing reflect true screening prevalence rather than a balanced curated dataset.

critiques.issue: No sample size or power calculation is provided — enrollment, expected referable prevalence, and per-subgroup/gate adequacy are all deferred to future statistician work, so the study is not yet powerable.
critiques.evidence_basis: Field 5 states 'No sample-size precedent was retrieved' (Confidence LOW); no prospective DR-screening trial with enrollment figures appears in the retrieved ClinicalTrials.gov/literature layer.

critiques.issue: Multiple mandatory subgroups (pigmentation/ethnicity, media opacity, camera vendor, DME, quality strata) are named but neither their thresholds nor their individual powering are specified, risking underpowered or post-hoc subgroup claims.
critiques.evidence_basis: Field 6 requests these be 'independently powered (or at minimum report with CIs)' but concedes 'no retrieved study stratified on these for this exact model'; no numeric plan is anchored.

critiques.issue: Calibration of ordinal grade probabilities is flagged as decision-critical but no calibration metric or acceptance criterion (e.g., calibration slope/ECE target) is pre-specified alongside the AUROC/kappa reporting.
critiques.evidence_basis: PMID 42078464 (calibration-focused foundation-model benchmark, 2026) is cited as emphasizing calibration, yet the plan sets no quantitative calibration acceptance bound.

panel.strength: The plan ties the output to a concrete clinical action (referable-DR flag → specialist review) and mandates monitoring of over-read override rate and referral yield, keeping workflow fit in view.

critiques.issue: Because the model is fundus-only, DME-driven referrals are a known blind spot that directly affects clinical action, yet the plan only proposes OCT confirmation 'where feasible' rather than defining how missed DME is handled in the referral pathway.
critiques.evidence_basis: Fusion literature (PMID 42346900 Fundus-OCT fusion, 2026; PMID 40171193 OCT+retinal-image meta-analysis, 2025) supports that fundus-only grading underdetects macular edema; the plan itself lists DME as a non-certified item.

critiques.issue: The ungradable-image gate is safety-critical for workflow, but the acceptable ungradable rate and the operational path for gated patients (repeat imaging vs. direct referral) are left to the team, creating alert/rework-fatigue risk in the target older, cataract-prone population.
critiques.evidence_basis: Field 2/3 defer the maximum acceptable ungradable rate to expert setting; media opacity/small pupils are noted as physiologic degraders specific to this diabetic population.

critiques.issue: Laterality/metadata integrity is a documented, clinically consequential failure mode (wrong-eye grade) that the monitoring plan addresses post-deployment but does not gate as a pre-launch acceptance criterion.
critiques.evidence_basis: Recall Z-2046-2013 (NAVIS-EX left-eye image saved as right eye) is the exact-modality failure mode cited; the plan treats it as monitoring rather than a pre-launch pass/fail check.

fix_before_submission: Lock the intended-use claim as autonomous diagnosis vs. screening-triage-with-mandatory-over-read before finalizing comparator and evidentiary bar.
Establish the applicable product code/pathway via FDA Pre-Submission; independently verify (do not assume) any autonomous DR-AI De Novo precedent since none was retrieved.
Compute a prospective sample size powering the lower CI of referable-DR sensitivity against locked thresholds and true screening prevalence, including the ungradable stratum.
Pre-specify subgroup definitions and per-subgroup powering/CI reporting for pigmentation/ethnicity, media opacity, camera vendor, DME, and quality strata (no post-hoc analysis).
Define how the fundus-only DME blind spot is handled in the referral pathway (e.g., OCT confirmation protocol) rather than leaving OCT as optional.
